steps.title: Use the square area scale factor
steps.content: The side length of square M is $5$ times the side length of square N. Since area scales by the square of the side-length scale factor, square M's area is $5^2=25$ times square N's area.

steps.title: Find the side length of square N
steps.content: The area of square N is 64 square feet. Since area equals side squared, the side of N is $$\sqrt{64} = 8$$ feet.

steps.title: Apply the area scale factor rule
steps.content: When linear dimensions scale by factor $k$, area scales by $k^2$. Since the side of M is 5 times the side of N, the area of M is $5^2 = 25$ times the area of N. Therefore, area of M = $$25 \times 64 = 1,600$$ square feet. That is choice A, the correct answer.
